days.title: Arrival in Guwahati – Drive to Bhalukpong (Distance: 250 km)
activities: Welcome to the gateway of Northeast India!
Upon arrival at Guwahati Airport or Railway Station, begin your drive to Bhalukpong, a tranquil town nestled on the Assam–Arunachal border, known for its natural beauty and river landscapes.
Overnight Stay in Bhalukpong

days.title: Bhalukpong to Itanagar
activities: Post breakfast, head to Itanagar, the capital of Arunachal Pradesh, a city that blends tribal traditions with modern culture.
Explore the peaceful Buddhist Temple and enjoy the calm of the capital city.
Overnight Stay in Itanagar
Places Covered: Bhalukpong, Itanagar, Buddhist Temple

days.title: Sightseeing in Itanagar | Drive to Ziro (Distance: 130 km)
activities: Begin your day with visits to Ganga Lake, a serene forest-surrounded lake, and the Jawaharlal Nehru State Museum, showcasing tribal heritage and artifacts.
Later, drive to Ziro, a UNESCO World Heritage Site famous for its scenic valley views and home to the Apatani tribe, known for their sustainable farming practices and unique facial tattoos.
Overnight Stay in  Ziro
Places Covered: Ganga Lake, Jawaharlal Nehru Museum, Ziro Valley

days.title: Exploring Ziro – Apatani Cultural Tour
activities: Start your day with an early village walk and tea at a local stall. Visit the paddy fields to witness pisci-culture, a rare practice of farming fish and rice together.
Explore Hapoli township, the commercial center of Ziro; Tarin Fish Farm, a high-altitude fish breeding center; and the local Tribal Museum.
Later, visit Hong village, Asia’s largest tribal village, and experience the rich traditions of the Apatani people.
Overnight Stay in Ziro
Places Covered: Paddy Fields, Hapoli, Tarin Fish Farm, Ziro Museum, Hong Village

days.title: Ziro to Tezpur (Distance: 160 km)
activities: After breakfast, head to Tezpur, an ancient city on the banks of the Brahmaputra, steeped in mythology and colonial-era charm.
Visit Mahabhairav Temple, believed to be built by King Bana; Cole Park, a green heritage park; and Agnigarh, a legendary fortress linked to Hindu mythology.
Overnight Stay in Tezpur
Places Covered: Mahabhairav Temple, Cole Park, Agnigarh, Tezpur

days.title: Tezpur to Guwahati – Departure
activities: Post breakfast, drive back to Guwahati, the largest city of Assam and the cultural and commercial hub of Northeast India.
Trip ends with unforgettable memories of Arunachal’s untouched beauty and vibrant tribal culture.

days.title: Arrival in Guwahati | Transfer to Bhalukpong (212 km)
activities: Arrive at Guwahati Airport/Railway Station, where our representative will greet you.
If time permits, you can visit the famous Kamakhya Temple, a revered Shakti Peeth.
Begin your journey towards Bhalukpong, a beautiful town at the Assam-Arunachal Pradesh border.
Reach Bhalukpong by evening and check in at the hotel.
Overnight stay in Bhalukpong.

days.title: Bhalukpong to Dirang (146 km)
activities: Wake up to a refreshing morning and enjoy breakfast.
Start your journey towards Dirang, a picturesque town known for its pleasant weather and stunning landscapes.
En route, you will witness the breathtaking beauty of the Kameng River.
Arrive in Dirang and check in at the hotel.
Spend the evening at leisure, exploring the quaint town.
Overnight stay in Dirang.

days.title: Dirang to Tawang (130 km)
activities: Post breakfast, depart for Tawang, a land of unparalleled natural beauty.

En route, you will visit Hot Springs, known for their medicinal properties, Sela Pass (13,700 ft), one of the highest motorable passes, offering mesmerizing views, Jaswantgarh War Memorial, dedicated to Rifleman Jaswant Singh Rawat, Nuranang Waterfalls, a stunning cascade near Dirang.
Arrive in Tawang and check in at the hotel.
Overnight stay in Tawang.

days.title: Tawang Local Sightseeing | Excursion to Bumla Pass
activities: After breakfast, explore the enchanting landscapes of Tawang.
Today you will visit PTSO Lake, a high-altitude glacial lake, Bumla Pass, the Indo-China border known for its historical significance, Sangetsar Lake (Madhuri Lake), famous for its surreal beauty.
Return to Tawang by evening and relax.
Overnight stay in Tawang.

days.title: Tawang to Bomdila (180 km)
activities: Post breakfast you will visit Tawang Monastery (Galden Namgyal Lhatse), the largest monastery in India.
Afterward, visit, Tawang War Memorial, dedicated to the heroes of the 1962 Indo-China war.
Depart for Bomdila, stopping by Kiwi Gardens en route.
Arrive in Bomdila and check in at the hotel.
Overnight stay in Bomdila.

days.title: Bomdila to Tezpur (155 km)
activities: After breakfast, visit Bomdila Monastery, offering panoramic views of the valley.
Drive towards Tezpur, stopping at the Orchid Research Center at Tipi en route.
If time permits, explore, Ganesh Ghat, a serene spot by the Brahmaputra Rive and Cole Park, known for its lush greenery and sculptures.
Overnight stay in Tezpur.

days.title: Tezpur to Guwahati | Departure (185 km)
activities: Enjoy breakfast and check out from the hotel.
If time allows, visit Maha Bhairab Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva and Agnigarh, a historic site offering scenic views of Tezpur.
Travel back to Guwahati and get dropped off at the Airport/Railway Station.
Trip Ends with Beautiful Memories!

days.title: Arrival | Guwahati to Bhalukpong
activities: Your journey begins in Guwahati, the gateway to Northeast India.
Enjoy a scenic drive to Bhalukpong, a charming town on the Assam-Arunachal border. The route takes you through lush green landscapes and along the pristine Jia Bharali River.
Once you arrive, check into your accommodation and take the rest of the day to relax.
Stroll along the riverbanks, explore the local markets, or simply soak in the serene surroundings. This is your first step into the breathtaking landscapes of Arunachal Pradesh.

days.title: Bhalukpong to Dirang
activities: After breakfast, drive towards Dirang, a scenic hill town known for its pleasant climate and breathtaking valleys.
Your first stop is TDL Monastery, a peaceful Buddhist retreat with stunning views of the valley.
Then, head to Mandala Top, a mesmerizing high-altitude spot surrounded by 108 Buddhist stupas and a paradise for birdwatchers.
En route, stop at the Holy Waterfall, where crystal-clear water cascades down mossy rocks.
End the day with a visit to Dirang Monastery, known for its spiritual ambiance and Tibetan architecture. Spend the evening unwinding at your hotel.
Overnight stay in Dirang.
Places Covered: TDL Monastery, Mandala Top, Holy Waterfall, Dirang Monastery

days.title: Dirang to Tawang
activities: Gear up for an adventurous drive to Tawang, passing through some of the most stunning landscapes in Arunachal Pradesh.

Your first major stop is Sela Pass, standing tall at 13,700 feet and surrounded by snow-capped peaks. Just below lies the Sela Lake, a surreal high-altitude lake that remains frozen for most of the year.
Pay homage at the Jaswant Singh War Memorial, a tribute to a heroic Indian soldier.
Overnight stay in Tawang.
Places Covered: Sela Pass, Sela Lake, Jaswant Singh War Memorial

days.title: Exploring Tawang
activities: Today, dive into Tawang’s history and natural beauty.
Visit the Bumla Pass, a high-altitude pass at the Indo-China border, offering breathtaking views.
Next, head to Madhuri Lake, a stunning lake surrounded by snow-capped mountains and pine trees (renamed after actress Madhuri Dixit when a Bollywood film was shot here).
In the afternoon, explore Tawang’s bustling local markets like Nehru Market, Old Market, and New Market, where you can shop for traditional crafts, Monpa woolens, and souvenirs.
Overnight stay in Tawang.
Places Covered: Bumla Pass, Madhuri Lake, Nehru Market, Old Market, New Market

days.title: Tawang’s Cultural Side
activities: Begin the day at Tawang Monastery, the largest monastery in India and the second-largest in the world, home to over 400 monks and a giant golden Buddha statue. Walk to the Giant Buddha Statue, a towering golden figure overlooking Tawang.
Then, head to Chakzam Bridge, a 600-year-old iron suspension bridge built by a Tibetan monk. Visit the peaceful Kitpi Village and the beautiful Khinmey Monastery.
In the afternoon, explore Urgelling Monastery, the birthplace of the 6th Dalai Lama. Conclude the day at Tawang War Memorial, where a Light and Sound Show narrates tales of bravery from the 1962 war.
Places Covered: Tawang Monastery, Giant Buddha Statue, Chakzam Bridge, Kitpi Village, Khinmey Monastery, Urgelling Monastery, War Memorial & Light Show

days.title: Tawang to Bomdila
activities: After breakfast, depart for Bomdila, a picturesque town nestled in the Himalayas.
En route, stop at Nag Temple, a revered spot offering spiritual vibes and beautiful views.
Continue to Gontse Garden Rabgye Ling Monastery, where monks engage in prayer and meditation.
Another stop at the Jung Waterfalls gives you one last chance to admire Arunachal’s natural beauty.
Once in Bomdila, explore the Bomdila Craft Center & Museum, showcasing exquisite Thangka paintings and local handwoven carpets.
Places Covered: Nag Temple, Gontse Monastery, Jung Waterfalls, Bomdila Craft Center

days.title: Bomdila to Guwahati | Departure
activities: Enjoy a relaxed morning before heading back to Guwahati. The drive, about 8 hours long, is filled with scenic landscapes, giving you one last chance to admire the breathtaking beauty of Arunachal Pradesh.
Upon arrival, your trip concludes with a drop-off at your hotel or Guwahati Airport, marking the end of an unforgettable journey.
Important to Know:
Weather: Prepare for unpredictable weather, especially at high altitudes.
Altitude: Tawang’s high altitude can cause sickness; stay hydrated.
Road Conditions: Roads can be rough and travel time longer in some areas.
Connectivity: Limited mobile network in remote areas.
Permits: Special permits required for some locations (Tawang, Bumla Pass).
Packing: Bring weather-appropriate clothes, medications, and essentials.
Culture: Respect local customs, especially at religious sites.

days.title: Arrival at Dibrugarh – Transfer to Aalo
activities: Arrive at Dibrugarh Airport and begin your scenic drive toward Arunachal Pradesh.
Cross the iconic Bogibeel Bridge over the mighty Brahmaputra.
Journey through lush tea gardens and forested hills as you enter the hills of Arunachal.
Reach Aalo (Along) by evening, nestled by the Siang River.
Highlights of the Day: Bogibeel Bridge, Siang River, Aalo Town

days.title: Aalo to Mechuka
activities: Embark on a thrilling drive to the remote valley of Mechuka, near the Indo-Tibet border.
Pass through dramatic gorges, tribal hamlets, and mountain terrains.
Capture stunning vistas along the way as the altitude increases.
Check into your homestay by evening and rest.
Highlights of the Day: Mechuka Valley, tribal routes, mountain passes

days.title: Mechuka Sightseeing
activities: Start the day with a visit to the beautiful Samten Yongcha Monastery overlooking the valley.
Head to Mechuka Valley Viewpoint and explore the suspension bridge in Dorjeeling Village.
Wander through local Tibetan villages and interact with the Memba tribe.
Enjoy traditional cuisine and culture.
Highlights of the Day: Samten Yongcha Monastery, Mechuka Viewpoint, Memba Tribe

days.title: Mechuka – Local Exploration
activities: Enjoy a riverside walk along the pristine Siyom River.
Visit Guru Nanak Taposthan, a revered spiritual site.
Opt for short hikes or participate in a cultural experience at local homestays.
Relax amid Mechuka’s peaceful aura.
Highlights of the Day: Siyom River, Guru Nanak Taposthan, hiking and local culture

days.title: Mechuka to Pasighat
activities: Drive through lush hills, bamboo groves, and rugged terrain toward Pasighat.
Stop for photos at scenic bends or waterfalls along the route.
Reach the vibrant town of Pasighat by evening.
Unwind in this gateway town on the banks of the Siang.
Highlights of the Day: Mountain drive, Pasighat town, Siang River views

days.title: Pasighat to Roing
activities: Begin your drive to Roing, crossing villages like Silluk and Dambuk.
If time permits, visit Silluk – known for its cleanliness and flowers.
Cross the Dibang River before reaching Roing.
Relax and visit the local market or lakes in the evening.
Highlights of the Day: Silluk Village, Dibang River, Roing Lakes

days.title: Roing to Anini
activities: Leave early for a long yet rewarding drive to Anini, one of India’s most isolated towns.
Ascend through the Dibang Valley, passing through changing altitudes and vegetation.
Marvel at remote tribal settlements and panoramic views.
Arrive in Anini by late evening.
Highlights of the Day: Dibang Valley, Anini, high-altitude landscapes

days.title: Anini Sightseeing
activities: Visit the picturesque Mathun Valley and the serene Dri River confluence.
Explore traditional villages and gain insight into the Idu Mishmi tribe's way of life.
You can opt for short hikes around the surrounding forests.
Spend a peaceful evening soaking in Anini’s wilderness.
Highlights of the Day: Mathun Valley, Dri River, Idu Mishmi Tribal Culture

days.title: Anini to Roing
activities: Begin the return journey to Roing, passing once again through the lofty landscapes of Dibang.
Stop for photos and soak in different perspectives of the valley.
Arrive in Roing by evening and rest at your accommodation.
Evening at leisure for shopping or lakeside views.
Highlights of the Day: Return through Dibang Valley, Roing town

days.title: Roing to Chowkham via Parshuram Kund
activities: Head to Chowkham, known for its forested hills and Buddhist culture.
En route, stop at Parshuram Kund, a sacred site on the Lohit River, often visited by pilgrims.
Take in panoramic views and spiritual vibes.
Continue to Chowkham for the night.
Highlights of the Day: Parshuram Kund, Lohit River, Chowkham forests

days.title: Chowkham to Dibrugarh Airport
activities: After breakfast, begin your return journey to Dibrugarh Airport.
Bid farewell to the wild charm of Arunachal’s eastern belt.
Carry home unforgettable memories of one of India’s most untouched travel circuits.
Trip ends with stories of tribes, valleys, and remote frontiers.
Highlights of the Day: Return via Lohit Valley, Dibrugarh departure
